source_issue.title: Create a GitHub App that detects first-time comments and responds with links to how to contribute

summary_markdown:
## What this issue is about
This issue aims to create a GitHub App that automatically detects first-time comments on Plone repositories. The goal is to respond with helpful links to our contributing guidelines, ensuring that new contributors have the information they need to get started.

## What needs to be done
We need to develop a Probot application that identifies first-time commenters across all Plone repositories and sends them links to helpful resources. This involves implementing a bot that can read issues and post comments based on specific conditions.

## Helpful background
The issue arose from the increasing number of first-time comments that lack reference to our guidelines. Responding manually has become time-consuming. Probot is a useful library for creating GitHub Apps and has tutorials available to assist in the process. 

## Good first steps
1. **Fork the repository**: Create a fork of the `plone/documentation` repo.
2. **Set up locally**: Follow Probot's documentation to get a test version running with your GitHub account.
3. **Share your progress**: Once you have a working version, please open a pull request or share your code for review.
4. **Engage with the community**: Seek feedback from other contributors in the Plone community forum. 

Feel free to reach out if you have questions or need guidance!
